entries.summary: Sinema announced retirement as Super Tuesday primaries reshaped Senate landscape.
Maryland and West Virginia held Senate primaries cementing key general election matchups.
Al Jazeera explainer covered why only some Senate seats are up each cycle.
Al Jazeera takeaways included tight Senate battleground state dynamics this week.
Al Jazeera identified Senate races likely to decide party control of chamber.
Al Jazeera maps and charts explainer included House and Senate maps.
Al Jazeera simple guide covered presidential, House, and Senate stakes.
Al Jazeera explained how election day would unfold across federal races.
Polls opened across the US for presidential and congressional elections.
Republicans began flipping Senate seats including West Virginia and Ohio.
Al Jazeera reported Democrats said House was within grasp as transition began.
Al Jazeera reported Trump presidency taking shape while House hung in balance.
Republicans clinched House control, retaining their congressional trifecta with Senate.
Senate Republicans elected John Thune as their majority leader for next Congress.
119th Congress convened with Mike Johnson reelected as House Speaker.
Congress certified Trump's Electoral College win without major incident.
Sinema retired, creating Arizona Senate open seat for the fall election.
Bernie Moreno won Ohio Republican Senate primary in three-way race.
Maryland Senate primary set up Alsobrooks-Hogan general election matchup.
Andy Kim won New Jersey Democratic Senate primary uncontested.
Michigan Senate primary set up Slotkin-Rogers general election matchup.
Connecticut Senate primary held alongside other state primaries this Tuesday.
CNN published October Senate race rankings analysis ahead of November vote.
Voters cast ballots in thirty-four Senate races across the United States.
Republicans flipped West Virginia and Ohio Senate seats Tuesday evening.
Republicans declared Senate majority after winning at least 53 seats.
McCormick narrowly defeated Casey to flip Pennsylvania Senate seat for Republicans.
Republicans clinched House control, retaining their congressional trifecta.
Senate Republicans elected John Thune as their next majority leader.
119th Congress convened with Republican majorities in both chambers.
Mike Johnson reelected as House Speaker on the first ballot.
Congress certified Trump's Electoral College win without incident.
